title: Capacity, Observability, Support, and Production Operations · Stakeholder Workshop
summary: A guided product team extends operational readiness and capacity case while preserving every earlier problem, accessibility, security, testing, operations, and recovery requirement. Planned artifact: Build a complete first version of operational readiness and capacity case while every earlier competency remains active. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Model demand, concurrency, data, compute, memory, storage, network, third-party quotas, growth, bursts, and failure reserve with uncertainty.
Measure task availability, correctness, latency, freshness, durability, and support from the user boundary and correlate them with system signals.
Design logs, metrics, traces, profiles, audits, retention, access, redaction, sampling, cardinality, and cost around diagnostic questions.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.config:
# capstone-project cumulative independent product evidence portfolio
# Browser labs perform deterministic evidence-contract review only.
# They never contact participants or services, execute commands, deploy, or claim live conformance.
workspace: portfolio
project-id: capstone-project
stakeholder: [replace with affected people and accountable decision owner]
decision: [replace with the decision this evidence changes]
problem-evidence: [replace with observed need, counterevidence, and uncertainty]
accessibility: [replace with keyboard, structure, reflow, status, alternatives, and human review]
security: [replace with threat, authorization, abuse, response, and residual risk]
privacy: [replace with purpose, minimization, retention, access, correction, and deletion]
test-strategy: [replace with behavior, changed case, fault, accessibility, security, and recovery]
release-evidence: [replace with revision, artifact, inventory, provenance, validation, and rollback]
recovery: [replace with backup, clean restore, reconciliation, communication, and ownership]
portfolio-proof: [replace with contribution, limitations, counterexample, and unseen defense]
transfer-boundary: authorized controlled research, toolchain, deployment, and review environments
evidence:

steps.title: Commit to the mission model · Workshop Capacity Model
steps.instruction:
workshop case — Feature completion hides an unrealistic workload, system-centered metrics, inaccessible support, untested runbook, and no compound incident rehearsal. The workshop variation 5 changes the stakeholder, access need, evidence source, constraint, failure, and operating environment. Competency probe: Model demand, concurrency, data, compute, memory, storage, network, third-party quotas, growth, bursts, and failure reserve with uncertainty.

What must be established before implementation?

Evidence target: Commit to the mission model · Workshop Capacity Model.
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for model demand, concurrency, data, compute, memory, storage, network, third-party quotas, growth, bursts, and failure reserve with uncertainty. For Commit to the mission model · Workshop Capacity Model, keep Capacity Model connected to mechanically reviewable stakeholder, affected-user, artifact, changed-case, failure, repair, release, recovery, and transfer decision evidence.

content.text: Feature completion hides an unrealistic workload, system-centered metrics, inaccessible support, untested runbook, and no compound incident rehearsal. The workshop variation 5 changes the stakeholder, access need, evidence source, constraint, failure, and operating environment. Competency probe: Model demand, concurrency, data, compute, memory, storage, network, third-party quotas, growth, bursts, and failure reserve with uncertainty.

steps.title: Extend the cumulative artifact · Workshop Incident Game Day
steps.instruction:
Append a capstone-incident-game-day-tm6512: manifest entry with concrete verify plus failure, owner, or repair evidence.

Evidence target: Extend the cumulative artifact · Workshop Incident Game Day.
steps.why: Building a runnable or mechanically reviewable artifact turns Incident Game Day into observable performance evidence. For Extend the cumulative artifact · Workshop Incident Game Day, keep Incident Game Day connected to mechanically reviewable stakeholder, affected-user, artifact, changed-case, failure, repair, release, recovery, and transfer decision evidence.

content.code:
capstone-incident-game-day-tm6512:
  verify: [replace with a deterministic check]
  failure: [replace with the rejected condition]
  repair: [replace with a local reproduction and correction path]
content.caption: Shape of the required increment; replace generic language with the current stakeholder case.

content.text: The structural check is only one gate. The following changed-case decision and explanation must also hold.

steps.title: Defend the transferred model · Workshop Incident Game Day
steps.instruction:
Write the workshop evidence note for Incident Game Day: explain the governing condition, one failure, and how this concrete case was verified — Feature completion hides an unrealistic workload, system-centered metrics, inaccessible support, untested runbook, and no compound incident rehearsal. The workshop variation 4 changes the stakeholder, access need, evidence source, constraint, failure, and operating environment. content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Inject a realistic compound failure, observe detection, triage, communication, containment, recovery, reconciliation, and follow-up under time pressure.
